purpose: This repository implements a hooks service for the CRB's COS Lending Selling system, providing event notification capabilities. It serves as an integration point that receives notification events about loan sales and related activities and publishes them to a messaging system for downstream consumers.

apis.purpose: Health check endpoint for service monitoring
Receives notification payloads and publishes them as events to the messaging system

dataEntities.name: LoanSaleStatusChanged
dataEntities.description: Represents a change in loan sale status with loan details and financial information

dataEntities.name: BatchPurchaseCompleted
dataEntities.description: Represents a completed batch purchase with financial details and number of loans

dataEntities.name: TrueUpVolumeFeeCharged
dataEntities.description: Represents volume fee adjustments charged for a specific period

dataEntities.name: InvestorChanged
dataEntities.description: Represents a change in investor assignment for a loan

dataEntities.name: LoanTypeChanged
dataEntities.description: Represents a change in loan type classification

dataEntities.name: OverdueLoansAlert
dataEntities.description: Contains summary information about overdue loans

messagingPatterns.name: LoanSaleStatusUpdated
messagingPatterns.description: Event published when a loan sale status changes

messagingPatterns.name: BatchPurchaseCompleted
messagingPatterns.description: Event published when a batch purchase is completed

messagingPatterns.name: TrueUpVolumeFeeCharged
messagingPatterns.description: Event published when volume fees are adjusted/charged

messagingPatterns.name: LoanTypeChanged
messagingPatterns.description: Event published when a loan's type is changed

messagingPatterns.name: InvestorChanged
messagingPatterns.description: Event published when a loan's investor is changed

messagingPatterns.name: OverdueLoansAlert
messagingPatterns.description: Event published to alert about overdue loans

findings.title: OAuth/Authentication configuration exists but requires secure management
findings.description: The application uses OAuth for authorization with configuration stored in appsettings files. For production environments, ensure all secrets like connection strings and OAuth credentials are properly stored in secure secret management solutions like AWS Secrets Manager as mentioned in comments.

findings.title: Event schema changes may break subscribers
findings.description: The service publishes strongly-typed events to NServiceBus, but there's no versioning strategy evident in the code. Changes to event schemas could break downstream subscribers. Consider implementing event versioning and compatibility strategy.
